mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.0 percent within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a surge in consumer credit balances indicates a supply-demand mismatch where inventory buildup coincides with weakening demand, leading to margin compression and negative equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint index of INDPRO and RSAFS shows no predictive power for 60d returns (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment where the CPI index exceeds 300, aggressive federal funds rate hikes fail to anchor inflation expectations, causing real rates to spike and equity valuations to contract sharply over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns in high-CPI/high-rate periods do not significantly underperform low-CPI/low-rate periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts reflect sustained consumer confidence and credit availability, which historically lead positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as construction activity translates to broader economic momentum.
failure_condition: Rolling 1-year Spearman correlation between HOUST changes and 5d SPX returns remains below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly payroll employment growth exceeds 200,000 for two consecutive months, it signals an overheating labor market that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, ultimately depressing equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month change > 200000 for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX forward returns in months following strong payroll prints are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where consumer credit balances rise while industrial production falls indicates a shift from investment-driven to consumption-driven growth, which historically precedes equity drawdowns as corporate margins compress over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The spread between RSAFS and INDPRO growth rates shows no incremental predictive power for 20d returns beyond a single-series baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of supply chain stress where industrial production growth outpaces consumer credit expansion, inventory drawdowns force firms to raise prices, boosting nominal equity returns over the next 5 trading days as earnings revisions accelerate.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO growth rate > RSAFS growth rate
failure_condition: Forward returns in supply-constrained regimes do not exhibit a positive skew relative to demand-constrained regimes (t-test p > 0.05).
